comment_text.sn1.11:3.3: Naradeva is a term for a king. Here and at SN 9.6:4.3 it refers to the gods of the Thirty-Three, and at Thag 20.1:41.1 to Brahmā. At Cp 5:1.3 it apparently refers to the aristocrats who are “gods among men” (see Manu 11.82).
comment_text.sn1.11:3.4: Tidasa (“thirty”) is a poetic abbreviation for the “thirty-three” gods. Noted as such in Kṣīrasvāmin’s Amarakośodghāṭana 1.1.7.
comment_text.sn1.11:5.1: The vocative form bāle suggests that a female deity is being addressed, which is certainly the case in the parallel passage at SN 9.6:5.1. The commentary says that the speaker here is also a female deity.
comment_text.sn1.11:5.3: This famous verse, which occurs many times in the Suttas, was spoken by the Buddha at SN 15.20:8.2 and by Sakka, the lord of the thirty-three, at the Buddha’s passing (SN 6.15:6.2, DN 16:6.10.11. | In this context, saṅkhārā means “conditions” or “conditioned phenomena” rather than “volitional actions”, “choices”. | The unusual reading sabbasaṅkhārā (rather than vata saṅkhārā) recurs in the parallel passage at SN 9.6:5.3.

root_text.sn1.11:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn1.11:2.1: “Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, aññatarā tāvatiṁsakāyikā devatā nandane vane accharāsaṅghaparivutā dibbehi pañcahi kāmaguṇehi samappitā samaṅgībhūtā paricārayamānā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.11:4.1: Evaṁ vutte, bhikkhave, aññatarā devatā taṁ devataṁ gāthāya paccabhāsi:

translation_text.sn1.11:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn1.11:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.sn1.11:2.1: “Once upon a time, mendicants, a certain deity of the company of the thirty-three was amusing themselves in the Garden of Delight, escorted by a band of nymphs, and supplied and provided with the five kinds of heavenly sensual stimulation. On that occasion she spoke this verse:
translation_text.sn1.11:3.2: who don’t see the Garden of Delight!
translation_text.sn1.11:3.3: It’s the abode of lordly gods,
translation_text.sn1.11:4.1: When they had spoken, another deity replied with this verse:
translation_text.sn1.11:5.2: the saying of the perfected ones:
translation_text.sn1.11:5.4: their nature is to rise and fall;

variant_text.sn1.11:2.1: paricārayamānā → paricāriyamānā (sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed, mr)
